title: notifications core — background jobs
description: Core-owned jobs. Every send goes through SendNotificationJob (the orchestrator), which fans out per resolved channel via NotificationDispatched. Channel modules ship their own transport jobs (SendMailJob, SendSmsJob, SendPushJob, BroadcastInAppNotificationJob). Digests, expunge, reconcile, cache warm are core scheduled work.

jobs.queue: resolved-at-dispatch — notifications-critical if priority=critical, else notifications
jobs.purpose: The orchestrator. Called once per dispatch. Resolves preferences + digest mode + creates NotificationDelivery rows + fires NotificationDispatched per resolved channel. Channel modules pick up the event + send.
steps: Load Notification (created synchronously in the caller's transaction).
Resolve channels via PreferenceResolver: user preference → tenant default → category default.
For each channel: if digest_mode≠immediate + priority≠critical, delegate to DigestScheduler + fire DigestQueued. Else create NotificationDelivery row + fire NotificationDispatched with channel.
Notification.state transitions queued → sending as the first delivery flips.
jobs.backoff: n/a — orchestrator is thin, per-channel retries live in channel modules

jobs.purpose: Runs at each digest window boundary. Collects pending notifications for (user, category, channel, window), renders through the digest template, dispatches, marks items digest_delivered_at.
jobs.notes: Skips delivery if the aggregated digest is empty (no empty emails). Priority=critical notifications are NEVER in a digest — they bypass batching in SendNotificationJob.

jobs.purpose: Hard-deletes soft-deleted Notification rows past their retention window (30d after user archive OR 90d after cold-archive threshold). Emits audit rows in audit_log tier before delete so the trail survives.
jobs.notes: Batched by tenant to spread load; scheduled daily 02:15 UTC.

jobs.purpose: For each provider (mail/sms/push), poll the channel module's provider webhook backfill endpoint OR the provider's own event API for any missed events (Mailgun sometimes drops webhooks). Reconciles NotificationDelivery states.
jobs.notes: Delegates provider-specific reconciliation to channel modules via ReconciliationContract.

jobs.purpose: Refresh the cached recipient payload (email, phone, locale, timezone) for a user after UserUpdated. Cached to avoid a User query per dispatch.
jobs.notes: Save-storm collapse via uniqueFor=60s — a user rapidly editing profile fields only refreshes the cache once per minute.

critical.note: Security alerts, MFA challenges, safeguarding. NEVER opted out.

transactional.note: Invitations, receipts, first-touch. Cannot opt out per CAN-SPAM §7702(17) but digest still applies.

marketing.note: Always opt-in. Never on by default.
